finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_06.md:111 — Narrator gloss now reads "which was how she still talked about Michael more than twenty years after he'd moved to Seattle — present tense for his opinions, as though his taste in lawn ornaments could be evaluated from three thousand miles away." (3) "more than twenty years" is consistent with Nora being 34 and the father leaving when she was approximately 12. (4) Diane's present-tense speech habit preserved — "Your father would have *loved* it" retained unchanged, the conditional working for absence.
